payload.answer: Reject the proposed 500 memory cards. A CSV with 500 rows should not be atomized into individual durable memory cards without evidence that each row is a stable, user-relevant memory. This would likely create noisy, brittle, and non-personal records. Preserve the dataset externally or summarize only durable, user-relevant preferences/facts if explicitly supported by the source.
citations: Input: LLM proposes 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.

entity_resolution.reason: No specific durable user/person entity or stable personal fact was provided; the proposal is a bulk table-to-memory atomization.

payload.intent: Evaluate whether to commit a proposed bulk import of 500 memory cards, one per CSV row.

details: Blocked bulk atomization of a large table into 500 memory cards.
No durable personal memory cards were committed.
No current facts were asserted from the unseen CSV rows.

repair_options: Do not commit any row-level cards by default.
Ask the user what, if anything, from the CSV should be remembered long term.
Store the CSV as an external document or reference, not as personal memory cards.
If memory is needed, create a small number of high-level summary cards only when the summary contains durable, user-relevant facts.
Require explicit justification and source context for any individual row promoted to durable memory.
